text:
Testing is a crucial part of software development. Two common types are unit tests and integration tests. Unit tests check small pieces of code, like a single function, to make sure they work correctly on their own. Integration tests check how different parts of the system work together.

A popular approach is test-driven development, or TDD, where you write a test before writing the code itself. This helps you focus on what the code should do. When the test passes, you know the code meets the requirement.

Overall, a good testing approach gives developers confidence. They can make changes and add new features without worrying about breaking something that already works.
